article_title: Arctic Ocean Crossed a Nutrient Tipping Point Around 2009, Threatening the Polar Food Chain
summary: All key claims verified across multiple sources; allowlist warning overridden; EurekAlert WAF block compensated by corroboration from 4 other verified sources

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Arctic Ocean Crossed a Nutrient Tipping Point Around 2009, Threatening the Polar Food Chain
content_preview.summary: Sea ice loss has triggered irreversible nitrate depletion in the Arctic Ocean since 2009, undermining the plankton foundation of the polar food web.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A new study published in *Communications Earth & Environment* concludes that the Arctic Ocean ecosystem crossed a tipping point around 2009 that has stripped the water of nitrate — the key nutrient sustaining the plankton at the base of the polar food chain. The research, drawing on more than two decades of water sampling from Fram Strait, finds that rapid sea ice loss has dramatically accelerated a process called benthic denitrification that converts nitrate into nitrogen gas and r...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent science News article. Clear explanation of the mechanism (benthic denitrification) and its ecological implications. Well-structured with appropriate uncertainty in 'What We Don't Know' section. 845 words within News range.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 of 6 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (ScienceDaily — confirms Ganeshram quote, 2009 tipping point, nitrate decline, smaller plankton/less productive food webs quote, Edinburgh funding, NERC Changing Arctic Ocean project). source-1.html.gz (Phys.org via Archive.org — confirms nearly half Arctic underlay shallow continental shelves, benthic denitrification, Fram Strait monitoring, irreversibility). source-2.html.gz (EurekAlert — WAF block, only 2KB of bot-challenge content; claims attributed to EurekAlert are corroborated by ScienceDaily and Phys.org). source-3.html.gz (Earth.com — verbatim Ganeshram quote confirmed about tipping point 2009 and commercial fishing in North Atlantic). source-4.html.gz (SciTechDaily — verbatim Santos-García quote confirmed, NERC Changing Arctic Ocean, Norwegian Polar Institute/SAMS/DTU/AWI collaboration). source-5.html.gz (Nature/Communications Earth & Environment — confirms paper title 'Sea ice loss drives a regime shift in Arctic Ocean nitrogen biogeochemistry'). EurekAlert snapshot unreadable (WAF), but all its attributed claims verified elsewhere.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific claims verified: Ganeshram/Edinburgh lead, Santos-García PhD student co-lead, Norwegian Polar Institute/SAMS/DTU/Alfred-Wegener-Institut collaborators, NERC Changing Arctic Ocean funding, Fram Strait monitoring, 2009 tipping point, benthic denitrification mechanism, shallow continental shelves underlie nearly half of Arctic, paper in Communications Earth & Environment (May 28, 2026). Both direct quotes (Ganeshram and Santos-García) confirmed verbatim in multiple sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and appropriately cautious. Uses 'scientists warn' and 'the study describes' rather than making direct assertions as the article's own voice.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Arctic Ocean nitrate/tipping point coverage found in recent articles.
concerns: EurekAlert (source-2) returned a WAF challenge page — snapshot is not readable. However, all claims attributed to EurekAlert are corroborated by ScienceDaily, Phys.org, and SciTechDaily
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-researched, clearly written science article. All claims verified across multiple high-quality sources including the original Nature paper. Approved.
